_why: Governs RAG behaviour for AI-OS runtime. Defines retrieval triggers, ranking, freshness, citation policy, and fallback strategy.

retrieval_policy._why: RAG is not free. Each retrieval call costs latency. Only trigger when a loaded collection is likely to improve answer quality over model knowledge.
auto_trigger_conditions: query contains library or API name with version specificity
query is about a codebase loaded as a collection
query asks for historical data or benchmark results
query asks 'how does X work in this project'
query contains 'according to the docs' or equivalent
skip_conditions: query is conceptual with no document-specificity
query is about current events (use web search instead)

ranking_policy._why: Multiple retrieval signals must be combined. Recency matters for versioned APIs but not for stable algorithms.

freshness_policy._why: API and library documentation changes frequently. Cached chunk scores from old ingestion runs may be misleading.
freshness_policy.freshness_action_on_warning: append [possibly stale — re-ingest recommended] to citation
freshness_policy.freshness_action_on_reject: skip chunk, fall back to model knowledge

chunk_strategy._why: Chunk size balances context quality against token budget. Large chunks carry more context but consume more tokens per retrieval.

retrieval_budget._why: Hard ceiling prevents RAG context from overwhelming conversation context.

citation_policy._why: Citations are mandatory when presenting RAG content. They allow the user to verify the source and detect stale information.
mandatory_when: any RAG content is present in the response

fallback_policy._why: When RAG retrieval fails or scores are below threshold, the system must not hallucinate. Model knowledge with explicit flagging is safer than silence.
fallback_policy.score_below_threshold: fall back to model knowledge, append [model knowledge — verify with current docs]
fallback_policy.retrieval_error: acknowledge error, state what data is missing, proceed with model knowledge
fallback_policy.no_collection_loaded: skip RAG entirely, use model knowledge

web_search_override._why: Web search is more current than any RAG collection. Use it when freshness is critical and no trusted collection covers the query.
override_conditions: query is about a software release in the last 30 days
query is about current pricing or availability
query is about breaking news or incidents
prefer_rag_conditions: a project-specific collection is loaded and relevant
query is about internal architecture or codebase
